question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 80.00% on 2026-04-23 for the Metaculus question "In Louisiana v. Callais, will SCOTUS strike down Louisiana’s two majority-Black districts, restoring a GOP-leaning map?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus community prediction on Louisiana v. Callais will be strictly above 80.00% on April 23, 2026. The CP has been at exactly 80.00% for 5 of 6 weekly snapshots (March 9 through April 13), dipping to 76% once and never exceeding 80%. The threshold requires strictly above 80.00%, making the status quo a NO resolution. Two pathways to YES exist: (1) SCOTUS decides the case before April 23 and strikes down the map (~10% probability of this happening, with ~82% chance they strike down if they decide), contributing ~8% to YES; (2) The CP drifts above 80% without a SCOTUS decision (~22% probability conditional on no decision), contributing ~19%. The case hasn't been decided yet despite being one of only 2 remaining October-session cases. Strong signals suggest SCOTUS will limit the VRA (Alito's Malliotakis concurrence, oral argument tone), but this information appears already priced into the stable 80% CP. With 81 forecasters converged at 80% and no clear near-term catalyst, I estimate ~30% probability of YES.

factors.description: CP has been at exactly 80.00% for 5 of 6 weekly snapshots (March 9 - April 13), never above 80%. Status quo resolves NO.

Strong signals SCOTUS will limit VRA: Alito's Malliotakis concurrence labeled race-conscious redistricting 'unadorned racial discrimination'; SCOTUSblog analysis suggests Alito writing Callais majority opinion.
Case could be decided before April 23: one of only 2 remaining October-session cases, pending 6 months since reargument. If SCOTUS strikes down map, underlying resolves YES → this resolves YES.
81 forecasters converged at 80%, creating a large stable base resistant to movement without a major catalyst.
Alito's Malliotakis concurrence and SCOTUSblog analysis are already priced into the current 80% CP. No clear new catalysts expected in the next 10 days.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% probability (YES)**

Key reasoning:

- **Status quo favors NO**: The CP has been at exactly 80.00% for 5 of 6 weekly snapshots and has never exceeded 80%. At exactly 80.00%, the question resolves NO.
- **Two pathways to YES**: (1) SCOTUS decides the case before April 23 and strikes down the map (~8% combined probability), or (2) CP drifts marginally above 80% (~19% probability).
- **Signals already priced in**: Alito's strong Malliotakis concurrence (March 2) and SCOTUSblog analysis suggesting he's writing the Callais opinion are already reflected in the stable 80% CP.
- **Large, stable forecaster base**: 81 forecasters have converged, making the CP resistant to movement without a major catalyst.
- **SCOTUS decision unlikely before April 23**: While Callais is one of only 2 remaining October-session cases, major constitutional decisions typically come in May-June.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) In Louisiana v. Callais, will SCOTUS strike down Louisiana’s two majority-Black districts, restoring a GOP-leaning map?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/40417)
[Is Justice Alito jumping the gun on voting rights?](https://www.scotusblog.com/2026/03/is-justice-alito-jumping-the-gun-on-voting-rights/)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/40417) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-23 12:47:38 is higher than 80.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 80.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-23 12:47:38, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
